title: Arc3D Object
description: Transient 3D arc. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient 3D arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 3D arc information. They are created statically using one of the create methods of the Arc3D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Arc3D object.

methods.name: createByCenter
methods.description: Creates a transient 3D arc object by specifying a center point and radius.

methods.name: createByThreePoints
methods.description: Creates a transient 3D arc by specifying 3 points. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 3D arc information.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the arc.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the arc.

methods.name: setAxes
methods.description: Sets the normal and reference vectors of the arc.

properties.name: asNurbsCurve
properties.description: Returns a NURBS curve that is geometrically identical to the arc.

properties.name: center
properties.description: Gets and sets the center position of the arc.

properties.name: curveType
properties.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

properties.name: endAngle
properties.description: Gets and sets the end angle of the arc in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

properties.name: endPoint
properties.description: Gets the end point of the arc.

properties.name: evaluator
properties.description: Returns an evaluator object that lets you perform additional evaluations on the curve.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: normal
properties.description: Gets the normal of the arc.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets and sets the radius of the arc.

properties.name: referenceVector
properties.description: Gets the reference vector of the arc.

properties.name: startAngle
properties.description: Gets and sets the start angle of the arc in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

properties.name: startPoint
properties.description: Gets the start point of the arc.

related_samples.name: Get Circle and Arc Data from Edge API Sample
related_samples.description: Display the arc and circle geometric information from a selected circular edge.

classType.title: Arc3D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.Arc3D.classType()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::Arc3D::classType();

classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Arc3D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Arc3D object.

syntax.python: returnValue = arc3D_var.copy()
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = arc3D_var->copy();

copy.return_description: Returns a new Arc3D object that is a copy of this Arc3D object.

createByCenter.title: Arc3D.createByCenter Method
createByCenter.description: Creates a transient 3D arc object by specifying a center point and radius.

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.Arc3D.createByCenter(center, normal, referenceVector, radius, startAngle, endAngle)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::Arc3D::createByCenter(center, normal, referenceVector, radius, startAngle, endAngle);

createByCenter.return_description: Returns the newly created arc or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center point of the arc.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: The normal vector of the arc. The plane perpendicular to this normal at the center point is the plane of the arc.

parameters.name: referenceVector
parameters.description: A reference vector from which the start and end angles are measured from. This vector must be perpendicular to the normal vector.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the arc.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The start angle in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

parameters.name: endAngle
parameters.description: The end angle in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

createByThreePoints.title: Arc3D.createByThreePoints Method
createByThreePoints.description: Creates a transient 3D arc by specifying 3 points. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 3D arc information.

syntax.python: returnValue = adsk.core.Arc3D.createByThreePoints(pointOne, pointTwo, pointThree)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = adsk::core::Arc3D::createByThreePoints(pointOne, pointTwo, pointThree);

createByThreePoints.return_description: Returns the newly created arc or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: pointOne
parameters.description: The start point of the arc.

parameters.name: pointTwo
parameters.description: A point along the arc. This point must not be coincident with the first or third points. This point must not lie on the line between the first and third points.

parameters.name: pointThree
parameters.description: The end point of the arc. This point must not be coincident with the first or second points.

getData.title: Arc3D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the arc.

syntax.python: (returnValue, center, normal, referenceVector, radius, startAngle, endAngle) = arc3D_var.getData()

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The output center point of the arc.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: The output normal vector.

parameters.name: referenceVector
parameters.description: The output reference vector.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The output radius of the arc.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The output start angle in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

parameters.name: endAngle
parameters.description: The output end angle in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

related_samples.name: Get Circle and Arc Data from Edge API Sample
related_samples.description: Display the arc and circle geometric information from a selected circular edge.

set.title: Arc3D.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the arc.

syntax.python: returnValue = arc3D_var.set(center, normal, referenceVector, radius, startAngle, endAngle)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = arc3D_var->set(center, normal, referenceVector, radius, startAngle, endAngle);

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The center point of the arc.

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: The normal vector of the arc. The plane perpendicular to this normal at the center point is the plane of the arc.

parameters.name: referenceVector
parameters.description: A reference vector from which the start and end angles are measured from. This vector must be perpendicular to the normal vector.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The radius of the arc.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The start angle in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

parameters.name: endAngle
parameters.description: The end angle in radians. This angle is measured from the reference vector using the right hand rule around the normal vector.

setAxes.title: Arc3D.setAxes Method
setAxes.description: Sets the normal and reference vectors of the arc.

syntax.python: returnValue = arc3D_var.setAxes(normal, referenceVector)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = arc3D_var->setAxes(normal, referenceVector);

parameters.name: normal
parameters.description: The new normal vector.

parameters.name: referenceVector
parameters.description: The new reference vector from which the start and end angles are measured from. The reference vector must be perpendicular to the normal vector.

transformBy.title: Arc3D.transformBy Method

syntax.python: returnValue = arc3D_var.transformBy(matrix)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = arc3D_var->transformBy(matrix);

transformBy.return_description: Return true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 3D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the curve.
